title: Linear Regression (Forward Pass)
description: Implement the forward pass of linear regression. Given inputs X (n × features), weights w, and bias b, compute predictions y_hat = X @ w + b.
key_concept: y_hat = X @ w + b. Matrix multiply input features by weights, add bias term. This is the prediction step before computing loss.

hints.L1: Linear regression prediction: y = X @ w + b. Use numpy matrix multiplication. X has shape (n, features), w has shape (features,), b is scalar.
hints.L2: import numpy as np. y_hat = np.dot(X, w) + b. Each output is the dot product of one sample's features with weights, plus bias.
hints.L3: def predict(X, w, b): return np.matmul(X, w) + b  # shape: (n,)

optimal_solution:
import numpy as np

def linear_regression_forward(X: np.ndarray, w: np.ndarray, b: float) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    X: shape (n_samples, n_features)
    w: shape (n_features,)
    b: scalar bias
    returns: y_hat shape (n_samples,)
    """
    return np.dot(X, w) + b
